title: Guru ka Tal
tagline: Guru ka Tal — heritage city of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Guru ka Tal is a historical Sikh pilgrimage place dedicated to the memory of ninth Guru Sri Guru Tegh Bahudar Ji.
overview.description: Guru ka Tal is a historical Sikh pilgrimage place dedicated to the memory of ninth Guru Sri Guru Tegh Bahudar Ji. Guru ka Taal is near Sikandra, in Agra. The Gurudwara was built over the place where the Guru Tegh Bahadur offered voluntary arrest to Aurangazeb, the Mughal Emperor. Several devotees gather every year to pay homage to the great Sikh guru (who was martyred along with his followers for sake of freedom to practice one's faith) in this Gurudwara.

topPlaces.name: Kakraita
topPlaces.description: Kakraita, also known as Kakrahta or Kakrentha, is a village of Sikandra block of Agra District in Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Akbar's tomb
topPlaces.description: Akbar's tomb is the mausoleum of the third and greatest Mughal emperor Akbar. The tomb was built in 1605–1613 by his son, Jahangir and is situated on 119 acres of grounds in Sikandra, a suburb of Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Tomb of Mariam-uz-Zamani
topPlaces.description: The Tomb of Mariam-uz-Zamani or Mariam's tomb is the mausoleum of Mariam-uz-Zamani, commonly known by the misnomer Jodha Bai, the favorite wife of the Mughal Emperor Akbar. The tomb was built by her son Jahangir, in her memory between years 1623–1627 and is located in Agra's Sikandra suburb next to Akbar's tomb, in the direction of Mathura.

topPlaces.name: List of tombs of Mughal Empire
topPlaces.description: Mughal tombs are a set of tombs built by various ruling in Mughal Emperors. All of them have marked influence from Iranian Timurid forms.

image.alt: List of tombs of Mughal Empire

topPlaces.name: 2011 Agra bombing
topPlaces.description: There was a blast in Indian city Agra on 17 September 2011 around 5:45 PM, at Jai Hospital, At least 15 people were injured and no fatalities were reported. Uttar Pradesh DGP and chief secretary to the Union home ministry said that due to the edge competition between two private hospitals the blast might have happened.

topPlaces.name: Swamibagh
topPlaces.description: Soamibagh is a town and a nagar panchayat in Agra district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Sanjay Place
topPlaces.description: Sanjay Place, is a Business District in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is a big market of electrical, communication, electronic items.

topPlaces.name: Akbar's Church
topPlaces.description: Akbar's Church also known as Church of Akbar is a Roman Catholic Church, built in 1600 by Jesuit Fathers, situated in Agra, India.

items.note: Kakraita, also known as Kakrahta or Kakrentha, is a village of Sikandra block of Agra District in Uttar Pradesh, India.

Akbar's tomb is the mausoleum of the third and greatest Mughal emperor Akbar. The tomb was built in 1605–1613 by his son, Jahangir and is situated on 119 acres of grounds in Sikandra, a suburb of Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India.

The Tomb of Mariam-uz-Zamani or Mariam's tomb is the mausoleum of Mariam-uz-Zamani, commonly known by the misnomer Jodha Bai, the favorite wife of the Mughal Emperor Akbar. The tomb was built by her son Jahangir, in her memory between years 1623–1627 and is located in Agra's Sikandra suburb next to Akbar's tomb, in the direction of Mathura.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Guru ka Tal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Guru ka Tal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Guru ka Tal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Guru ka Tal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Guru ka Tal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Guru ka Tal?
faq.a: Stays in Guru ka Tal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Guru ka Tal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kakraita, Akbar's tomb, Tomb of Mariam-uz-Zamani, List of tombs of Mughal Empire, 2011 Agra bombing.
